question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where path is an extremely long string, causing path + '?' + normalizedQuery to allocate a very large string in memory, exhausting heap memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where both path and normalizedQuery are maximally large, causing the final string concatenation 'path + '?' + normalizedQuery' to allocate a string exceeding available heap memory, causing a non-network DoS?
